XD介绍部分

04 / 96

About XD Inc.

05 / 96
2003 Founded
HKEX Listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange
1,300+ Employees

XD Inc. is a global video game developer and publisher with extensive experience in development, publishing, and live operations. Its portfolio includes self-developed titles such as GoGo Muffin, Torchlight: Infinite, and Heartopia, as well as published hits including Sausage Man and Dave the Diver.

As a company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, XD currently has over 1,300 employees.

TapTap介绍部分

08 / 96

Introducing TapTap

09 / 96

TapTap, a subsidiary of XD Inc.

TapTap, a subsidiary of XD Inc. founded in 2016, is China's leading Android game store with 45M+ monthly active users. It is a comprehensive gaming hub integrating distribution, media, and community, offering 300K+ titles across mobile and PC with 0 platform fee.

Official Website: www.taptap.cn

2025 Business Overview

10 / 96

Since 2016, TapTap has grown from 748 developers and 12M downloads to become China's leading Android games platform, with the largest registered developer base and a dominant position in the premium games segment.

Audience
45M+

Monthly Active Users

Large-scale community traffic across the platform.

Creator Base
250K+

Registered Developers

The largest registered developer base on the platform.

Demand
104M+

Pre-orders

Strong launch intent built through community anticipation.

Distribution
802M+

Downloads & Redownloads

Deep reach supported by repeat acquisition and retention.

Premium
6.7M+

Copies of Paid Games Sold

Proof of TapTap's leadership in the premium games segment.

The Vision

"In a fragmented Android world without Google Play, TapTap rebuilds the trust between players and developers to make discovering superb games easier than ever."

12 / 96

The Vision

12 / 96
From platform friction to trust-first distribution

TapTap resolves the four structural frictions developers face in China's fragmented Android channel landscape.

Traditional Android Stores

High platform friction, weak trust loops, and monetization-led traffic allocation.

Shift

TapTap's Model

Lower cost, cleaner incentives, and community-led discovery that rebuilds trust.

30%-50% Revenue share tied to channel access
vs
0% Commission, with an ad-driven business model
Heavy SDK burden Fragmented integration and operations overhead
vs
Optional integration No forced co-publishing or redundant integration layers
Low trust Fake rankings and unreliable review signals
vs
Real community signals Ratings, rankings, and feedback shaped by players
Traffic bias Distribution favors the highest-revenue titles
vs
Quality-first discovery Resource allocation designed around game quality and fit
Why it matters
TapTap removes four core frictions, then turns trust into the discovery engine.
Lower Cost Higher Trust Fairer Discovery

Platform Capabilities

17 / 96

Mobile

  • Integrates game store distribution and community.
  • Supports sandbox play, mini games, and cloud gaming.
  • TapTap SDK offers one-stop operations and compliance tooling.

PC

  • Sells Steam Keys through the PC tab in the mobile app.
  • Enables direct purchase and play on TapTap PC.
  • Supports cross-save between mobile and PC.

TapTap Maker

  • AI-native creation engine.
  • Built-in online multiplayer features.
  • One-click publishing to TapTap.
  • Free to use and easy to monetize.

Marketing: TapTap Official Events

18 / 96

TapTap Game Awards

TapTap's annual event celebrating the year's best games through both editorial recognition and player acclaim.

It serves as a high-visibility industry benchmark that spotlights standout titles and amplifies premium game buzz across the platform.

TapTap Game Festival

A summer showcase for upcoming titles, combining world premieres, curated reveals, and hands-on demo moments for core players.

The festival creates a forward-looking carnival atmosphere that helps connect gamers with the next wave of high-potential releases.

Spotlight Game Jam

An intensive creator challenge where developers build innovative prototypes in short cycles, helping TapTap surface new gameplay talent.

Run across multiple cities and formats, the program has become a practical funnel for identifying promising teams and incubating standout concepts.

China does not need Fortnite to explain what a shooter is. The challenge is to frame Fortnite as something broader than a traditional shooter.
Genre Evolution

A major gameplay breakthrough arrives every 5-8 years.

28 / 96
1999-2000
Round-Based Competitive Play Takes Shape

Counter-Strike 1.6 established the buy weapons → round attack/defense → economy loop. CrossFire brought that loop to China in 2007 and remains an evergreen operation today, with PC MAU still at 40 million.

2017-2018
Battle Royale Breaks Out

PUBG ignited the global market, while Game for Peace captured the China opportunity. Battle royale pushed shooters from hardcore competition into the mass market.

2022-2023
Extraction Emerges and Validates

Arena Breakout brought Tarkov-style extraction to mobile and validated it at mass-market scale, completing the genre's onboarding phase.

2024-2025
Extraction Hits Mass-Market Breakout

Delta Force surpassed 41 million DAU, while total extraction DAU reached 55 million. Game for Peace: Metro Royale at 37 million DAU proves that a mass-scale title can migrate into a new genre through a side mode.

Genre Landscape

A major genre shift: extraction is taking share from battle royale.

29 / 96

2024 Genre Share

Source: Diandian 2024 official data + top-title back-calculation

Genre Landscape

Battle royale educated the market. Extraction is now driving the next growth wave in survival shooters.

30 / 96

Genre Share Shift (2024→2025)

Source: Diandian 2024 official data + 2025 blended industry estimate
Battle Royale (BR)
43% → 36%
-7ppt | Officially reported for 2024; classic-mode growth slowed in 2025 for Game for Peace
Extraction
14% → 33%
+19ppt | Officially reported for 2024; Delta Force, Metro Royale, and Arena Breakout all broke out in 2025
Round-Based
~10% → ~12%
+2ppt | VALORANT expanded sharply across PC and mobile in 2025
Respawn
~9% → ~4%
-5ppt | Heavily cannibalized by extraction in 2025
All-Out Warfare
~2% → ~5%
+3ppt | Full-year support for all-out warfare modes in 2025 plus Battlefield Mobile
Other / PvE
~22% → ~10%
-12ppt | Reclassified into clearer gameplay buckets in 2025

China Market Takeaways × Our Opportunity
34 / 96

So where exactly should we enter?

Not another summary. A direct answer.

The opportunity is not to keep fighting in the crowded traditional shooter market, but to win through three shifts: perception, genre, and platform.
01
Perception Reset
Shift
From
the crowded traditional competitive shooter market
To
a youth entertainment and social market
Reframe Fortnite from “hardcore shooter” into “content consumption + light social + expressive entertainment.”
02
Genre Innovation
Shift
From
a race of pure development capability
To
competition around a UGC-driven creative ecosystem
Use “Shooter + X” to break out of the old lane and turn Fortnite’s edge into gameplay extensibility plus a creator ecosystem.
03
Platform Innovation
Shift
From
PC-led cross-platform extension
To
a mobile-first three-platform experience
Rebuild the experience around touch-first behavior, then let PC and console amplify value across scenarios.

41 / 96
42 / 96

WHAT IS
THREE-BODY?

China's Most Influential Sci-fi Story About Humanity Facing The Unknown.

After receiving a signal from Earth, a distant civilization begins preparing to come to our planet. As humanity realizes something unknown is approaching, the world falls into fear, conflict, curiosity, and massive change.
THE CORE OF THREE-BODY
Curiosity about the unknown
The feeling that something bigger is coming
First contact between worlds
Three-Body became a cultural phenomenon in China because it created a feeling larger than ordinary entertainment.

Phase 1 — Market Reactivation

Phase 2 — Hype & Reservation

Apr 2027 — Jun 2027 · 15%

Phase 3 — Launch Explosion

Jun 2027 — Jul 2027 · 50%

Turn the launch into a national cultural event.

Phase 4 — Long-Term Expansion

Aug 2027 — Jan 2028 · 30%

Sustain growth through ecosystem operations.

China Publishing Plan

THE SIGNAL HAS BEEN RECEIVED.

Welcome to Fortnite China.

66 / 97
Launch Readiness

Four things the China launch has to get right

Fortnite China's launch will hinge on four things: perception, monetization, platform fit, and creator support.

  • Refresh how players in China see Fortnite
  • Launch with a monetization model that feels attractive locally
  • Fit the product to China's cross-platform reality
  • Build a creator support plan for long-term live ops
67 / 97
Perception Reset

Chinese players still associate Fortnite with an older version of the product

The launch has to reset that perception.
Players still think of Fortnite as “battle royale + building”
Fortnite's cultural language still feels distant from local culture

What we do
What changes
New perception

a battle royale

→
Aligns with today's winning play pattern

The core mode feels more current

single-mode game

Expands the range of playable formats

Fortnite's memes
feel foreign

→
Connects with Chinese meme culture
→
Fortnite feels more familiar to Chinese players
69 / 97
Genre Opportunity

Extraction is now the clearest lead mode for a China launch

Three category leaders already validate demand

Delta Force
50M+ DAU
Game for Peace · Metro Escape
37M+ DAU
Supernatural Action Squad
10M+ DAU

How the category is evolving

1.0

Core loop established

Built on standard survival-shooter conventions and the core extraction loop.

  • Map memory: spawn points, extraction points, POIs
  • Loot value systems: collectables, gear tiers, value gaps
  • Trading layer: sell loot, buy equipment
2.0

Differentiation through theme and systems

Keep the extraction core, then layer in stronger themes and lightweight gameplay twists.

  • World / theme variants around festivals or supernatural hooks
  • Micro-innovation in operators, traversal, and mode-specific systems
3.0

Toward co-op-heavy extraction

Shift from pure resource contest toward stronger PVE cooperation, with PVP becoming more situational.

Current: strong PVP / weak PVE

Direction: stronger PVE, lighter direct PVP conflict. Reference cases include ARC Raiders and other co-op-oriented extraction systems.

70 / 97
Genre Design

Fortnite's extraction mode needs a clear Fortnite identity

First build a distinctive 2.0 extraction core, then explore broader co-op and PvPvE possibilities.

2.0

Define a differentiated extraction experience at launch

The first priority is to lock in a core loop that already feels distinct from standard extraction shooters before expanding the mode further.

A. Build the mode around worlds

  • Fortnite's own world
  • Partner IP worlds

B. Extraction + X

  • Building
  • Mobility
2.0 Benchmark
A distinct extraction rhythm, memorable map anchors, and meaningful in-match variables that clearly separate Fortnite from other extraction shooters.
3.0 comes later

Expand into broader co-op and PvPvE possibilities once the core is stable

Deepen PVE play content

Add more presence-driven environment challenges, bosses, control points, and event objectives.

Explore new PVP possibilities

Experiment with richer conflict, cooperation, betrayal, and extraction races inside the battlefield.

71 / 97
UGC Launch

The day-one UGC lineup should prove the format and start local content supply

Bring in proven global UGC formats

Pre-sign proven Chinese UGC teams

Elixir Ascension
Creator: Dai Xiaofan
Cultivation Monster Hunt Simulator
Creator: Xiaoyu
Shanhai Evolution
Creator: Youjia Technology
72 / 97
Cultural Expression

Fortnite's meme language has to start from references Chinese players already understand

Local meme expression works best when Fortnite draws from references Chinese players already know.
What to Localize
Connect emotes, jokes, and references to topics Chinese players are already talking about.
Why It Matters
Reduce cultural distance and make local players feel included faster.
What It Requires
Build a fast-response loop across live ops, marketing, and development so topical content can ship quickly.
73 / 97

Launch monetization should start with cosmetics that fit local taste

Players should be able to find a look that fits their taste and social identity right away.

  • Curate strong legacy skins for launch
  • Raise the share of East Asian-inspired skins
74 / 97
Payment Model

Direct purchase and the Battle Pass need local adaptation

Battle Pass
Direct-purchase shop
IP-driven value boost

Make the model easier to enter

Use a payment structure that feels familiar and easy to understand from day one.

Launch-time paid events

  • First top-up bundle
  • Limited-time discounts
  • Spend-and-reward events
75 / 97
Platform Strategy

The launch has to win mobile scale and PC credibility at the same time

China Shooter Split

Mobile drives scale. PC defines the category signal.

Mobile 75% Largest audience pool and broadest genre reach.
PC 25% Smaller base, but higher-value core users.
Mobile user scale is roughly 3x PC
Reach Mass-market coverage

Mobile carries the broad base of China's shooter category and sustains category-level reach.

Value Core shooter audience

PC remains the sharper signal for spending power, taste leadership, and market perception.

Approximate user split in China’s shooter market
Launch Implication

The launch has to win both scale and credibility.

PC drives value

PC is where the core shooter audience sits, with stronger spending power and more influence over market perception.

Mobile drives scale

Mobile carries the bulk of the category and delivers the reach needed to matter in China.

Treat mobile as the scale engine and PC as the credibility engine.
76 / 97
Mobile Adaptation

Mobile has to clear two bars: visual performance and download size

On visual performance, Delta Force is the benchmark.

Initial download should stay under 2 GB

Eggy Party
1.96 GB
Game for Peace
1.94 GB
Delta Force
1.51 GB

Game Content Creators

Keep playable content flowing

Creative

UEFN

Use quality content to reach more players outside the game and turn attention into fan communities.

Livestream
Real-time interaction and discovery
Video
Content distribution and retention

The Long-Term Live-Ops Engine

Ongoing content supply Always-on conversation Compounding community value
78 / 97
Creation Support

Creation support determines how many everyday players can become creators

Game for Peace · Oasis Genesis
58M+ DAU

Creation tools

Domestic products do more to help ordinary players and smaller teams start creating quickly.

Need to Match

Lower the creation threshold

Support mobile-side creation tools so creation can start before users become professional creators.

Explore

Blend creation tools with AI

Experiment with AI-generated assets and faster content assembly for smaller creator teams.

Eggy Party
40M+ DAU

Creator incentives

Leading domestic products rely more on cold-start exposure, event momentum, and diversified creator income.

Need to Match

Stronger support policies

Competition programs, traffic subsidies, and platform-side recommendation support.

Keep

More monetization options

Advertising revenue, player tipping, and other creator-friendly earning options.

Strategic Value For Epic

A customized, full-lifecycle, full-scenario TapTap ecosystem plan — difficult to replicate through conventional publishing channels.

89 / 97
China Publishing

ISBN Approval Strategy

Objective: obtain ISBN approval as quickly as possible.

Approach: separate the submission build from the live build, and submit for multiple platforms in parallel.

Submission build

Submission Build

Keep only the content needed to meet regulatory requirements and reduce revision cycles during review.

Live build

Live Build

Expand modes, features, characters, and live-ops content after approval based on the launch plan.

90 / 97
Submission Scope

Recommended Submission Build

Review-first scope

Get approval with the leanest submission build possible, then layer in broader content after launch.

The submission build should preserve Fortnite's core player appeal while keeping the review package as clean and manageable as possible.

Keep a limited set of core modes

The submission build can retain a focused set of first-party core experiences, such as battle royale or extraction.

Adjust based on XD's review guidance

Refine content based on XD's regulatory filing experience to reduce rework and review back-and-forth.

Save crossovers, licensed music, and UGC for later

Higher-sensitivity content such as IP collaborations, licensed tracks, and UGC features can be added through post-launch updates.

91 / 97
Publishing Capability

Why XD Has an Advantage in ISBN Filing

Track record
118
Approved titles since 2019

XD holds an Online Publishing Service License and has one of the strongest recent filing track records in the market.

Execution speed
6-8 months
Expected approval cycle

XD brings established regulatory coordination and filing experience, helping resolve issues more efficiently throughout the process.

+

The advantage is not just speed, but a more predictable and manageable approval process.

92 / 97
Compliance Stack

Core Compliance Capabilities, Ready to Integrate

Real-name verification and anti-addiction controls

TapTap SDK provides a proven solution for identity verification, playtime controls, and spending restrictions for both submission and live operations.

Content safety and moderation

Moderation models cover usernames, avatars, chat, and comments, enabling real-time detection and blocking of sensitive or non-compliant content.

A proven compliance stack can be integrated quickly, reducing the cost of building these systems from scratch.

93 / 97
UGC Governance

A Mature UGC Compliance Framework

Built on TapTap Maker and Heartopia's UGC operating experience, XD already has a well-developed framework for UGC governance.

End-to-End UGC Governance
Creation-stage safeguards
Pre-publish review
Key-area monitoring
External risk and sentiment monitoring
